raw_input:
[5.0/5] Soft & soothed skin I used this later in a day, after derma rollering, and I felt it helped soothe my skin. No lingering irritation or redness from the derma roller. I'm not sure if it was this serum alone or how it worked in conjunction with my other skin care products, but my skin felt velvety soft afterwards. I enjoyed the lavender-scented milky texture of this serum. It came in a sealed box. It's affordably priced at $9.99. I would get this again. [5.0/5] Super Serum! My wife has sensitive skin. There's a lot of products out there she really doesn't like. She loves this brand and loves this product. So does her skin!

No redness afterwards from irritation, feels so refreshing while being applied, isn't greasy, no tackiness after and smells wonderful. You can't go wrong with this brand

Also, super-fun-to-open-boxes seem to be a thing with Celleanser. Love that little detail when it comes to their presentation. We're thinking anything from their line would make an awesome gift because of it and you can feel good knowing the product itself is also going to work [4.0/5] It's good This is a nice pump serum, which I like better than those that use a dropper. Has a thick, milky texture and subtle medicinal scent that doesn't last long. Absorbs quickly and doesn't leave your skin feeling sticky, however, not much in the way of hydration until you add a cream! Like the other Celleanser products, ingredients listed in English on the box (if you open box by using the perforation, you'll see the ingredients and brand story inside, all written in Korean. It's reasonably priced, which is nice for those of us on a budget! [5.0/5] Loving this serum My skin is feeling hydrated, plumper and more radiant with this serum. It’s just giving soft beautiful skin. [5.0/5] Sensitive skin approved Simple, clean, scentless serum. This is sensitive skin approved. [5.0/5] It's a good serum - Glass skin finish
- Hydrating, mildly calming and cooling
- No scent
- The serum is a milky color and spreads out clear.
- No breakouts
- It's a little tacky and dries slowly because of the hydrating glass skin properties. If you're after this kind of product it could work for you--especially because this is a clean and vegan formula. [3.0/5] Starting to react, tiny itch on my face by left nostril. I have been using this for about 5-days. I replaced the current moisturizer to try this serum. I have dry and mature skin.

Results;
1)Started to get a small reaction on my left side nostril. Starting to itch. Tiny spot.
2)Face felt smooth after application
3)used only a larger size dime, for my face
4)not oily
5)faint scent of alcohol
6)light feeling. Not heavy on my face
7)feels clean
8)Vegan
9)Not tested on animals.

Negative:
1)pricey for the amount
2)Tiny itch starting to form, near left nostril on face

There are a lot of ingredients! Nevertheless, It is pricey but the top-5 ingredients besides water, are listed here:
1)Glycerin-good hydrator by pulling water to surface of skin; helping to “plump “ your skin
2)Propanediol- one of it’s uses is an emollient to help retain hydration and moisturize in skin.
3)Cetyl Ethylhexanoate - one uses is to help texture; protects skin and traps moisture. Bad is derived from palm oil (sustainability issues)
4)Sorbitol - a type of sugar that supposedly makes a formula less cloudy; attracts and hold moisture; stabilizer (helps hold formula together); Used to add sweetness to lip products.
5)Dipropolyene Glycol - a type of alcohol. A chemical that helps stabilize a formula with its texture and helps dissolve other substances; supposedly, enhances penetration into skin.

The list of ingredients go on forever. There is a warning: Do not apply it on a part where you have a scar, eczema or dermatitis. So, since I’m starting to form a tiny itch, I will stop using it. I will be going back to my moisturizer. 3-stars, for me. [5.0/5] A Very Nice Hydrating, Soothing, Barrier-Repairing Antioxidant Serum (Ingredients list is at the bottom of this review.)

I have really enjoyed using the Celleanser Vegan Clean Serum, and my skin seems to like it too. I also tried the Celleanser Clean Toner Mist, and the Celleanser Clean Cream. They all worked nicely for my dry, sensitive skin, and they worked nicely together.

This serum is creamy with a viscous gel-like texture that provides an extra layer of hydration, moisture, and antioxidants on the skin. It is applied before moisturizer. The serum is smooth and cooling when applied. It soaks in well, but it does leave a nice layer of extra hydration on the skin. It is not sticky, and it layers perfectly with other products. The scent is sort of lightly herbal or earthy, similar to many Korean skincare brands.

I will say that the finish isn't my go-to. It isn't really dewy or glowy. But I wouldn't call it extremely matte either. Maybe somewhere in-between, but more toward the matte side. I typically like to use dewy, glowy skincare, so I would probably use this product at night for that reason.

The packaging is very nice with a unique-looking, tall, slim plastic bottle with a very functional pump. A little goes a long way, and I imagine this bottle will last me a while. At $19.99 for 30ml / 1.01 fl. oz., it's a pretty good deal. Especially because the ingredient list is nice.

I haven't been using it long enough to speak on the long-term results, but I do like the serum so far, and I will definitely continue to use it. I would recommend this to anyone who likes the ingredient list and wants to give it a try.

A NOTE TO CELLEANSER: It would be wonderful to add accurate, full ingredient lists to all of your products listed on Amazon. It would go a long way toward helping your customers save time and make informed decisions without any hesitations or uncertainties. Thank you!

INGREDIENTS:

Water, Glycerin, Propanediol, Cetyl Ethylhexanoate, Sorbitol, Dipropylene Glycol, Camellia Sinensis Leaf Extract, Panthenol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Centella Asiatica Leaf Extract, Asiaticoside, Madecassic Acid, Asiatic Acid, Boerhavia Diffusa Root Extract, Dipotassium Glycyrrhizate (Lavender) Extract, Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Extract, Thymus Vulgaris (Thyme) Extract, Origanum Vulgare Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract, Rehmannia Chinensis Root Extract, Angelica Gigas Extract, Paeonia Lactiflora Extract, Astragalus Membranhuba Fruit Extract, Zizip Solanum Lycopersicum (Tomato) Fruit Extract, Potentilla Chinensis Extract, Taraxacum Officinale (Dandelion) Leaf Extract, Cornus Officinalis Fruit Extract, Narcissus Tazetta Bulb Extract, Lecithin, Cetearyl Olivate, Sorbitan Olivate, Butylene Glycol, Xanthan Gum,Sodium Acrylates Copolymer, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Adenosine, Carbomer, Tromethamine, Citric Acid, Disodium EDTA, Caprylyl Glycol, Hydroxyacetophenone, Ethylhexylglycerin, Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Oil, Linalool. [5.0/5] Feels great on. Love the whole line This packaging is wonderful and it really moisturizes well. This is key for the winter which is so dry in my area. I'm really thrilled to have this. [5.0/5] love this product This stuff works great at relieving dry and worn skin. it doesnt bother my skin at all and i really like the way it makes my skin feel. [5.0/5] Clean Serum Great product!! It’s soft and very moist!! A great cleaning serum to add to my daily skincare routine!! Have you with soft and soothing skin!! Love it [5.0/5] It's soft and moist! My skin is sensitive. It effectively prevents dryness with a soft and moist serum.
I think it's good for all skin including sensitive skin! [2.0/5] Not very good [[VIDEOID:e7eed70099e670a8745ffe90605aa8c9]] Smells herbal, cleanser is very runny. Leaves skin feeling stripped but also doesn't remove sunblock or make up. [5.0/5] Clean serum to be added for your skincare routine I have sensitive skin as MOST people identify themselves with… I get rash/acne when the skincare is not well fitting. I develop allergies to products if used for more than normal use. So it is pretty difficult to be like a nomad in going around for skincare items. I am glad I ran into this item randomly by its name of Vegan and Clean/soft materials. Only a few days of use but I don’t have any reactions to this as I would for normal new products. Go for it. [5.0/5] Great Product!! Love this product. I've been using it for about 2 weeks now and I can tell a big difference in the appearance and feel of my skin, it's reduced my pores and helped with my oily skin. Also, my skin feels so hydrated and feels so smooth. I can't give this product enough stars love it. I recommend this for any skin type! [5.0/5] Good This product makes your skin smooth, really good!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, the Celleanser Vegan Clean Serum receives mostly positive feedback, with the majority of reviewers giving it 5 out of 5 stars. Customers frequently praise its hydrating and soothing properties, noting that it leaves skin feeling soft, smooth, and plump. Many reviewers highlight that it works well for sensitive skin, causing little to no irritation or redness. The serum's milky texture, light feel, and subtle lavender or herbal scent are commonly mentioned as positives, along with the fact that it absorbs quickly without leaving skin feeling sticky or greasy.

Several reviewers appreciate the clean, vegan formula and the affordable price point, which ranges from around $9.99 to $19.99. The packaging is also noted as attractive and functional. A few customers mention that it pairs well with other Celleanser products and works nicely as part of a broader skincare routine.

On the negative side, one reviewer gave it 3 stars after experiencing a small itching reaction near the nostril after a few days of use, and noted that some ingredients, such as those derived from palm oil, raise sustainability concerns. Another reviewer gave it 2 stars, finding the scent too herbal and the texture too runny, and felt it left their skin feeling stripped. One reviewer also noted that the ingredient list inside the box is written in Korean, which may be inconvenient for some customers.
